name: Authenticate user

targetRestContract.notes: Uses Ardalis.ApiEndpoints (EndpointBaseAsync), NOT the MinimalApi.Endpoint pattern used by catalog endpoints. Route is declared via [HttpPost] attribute rather than AddRoute(). On success returns a JWT valid for 7 days signed with HMAC-SHA256. lockoutOnFailure is true — 5 consecutive failures lock the account (ASP.NET Identity default).

notes: Uses Ardalis.ApiEndpoints pattern (EndpointBaseAsync.WithRequest<T>.WithActionResult<T>), not the MinimalApi IEndpoint<IResult,TRequest,TDep> pattern used by all catalog endpoints.
lockoutOnFailure: true is passed to PasswordSignInAsync — the response IsLockedOut field will be true after too many failures.
JWT claims: ClaimTypes.Name (username) + ClaimTypes.Role for each role the user holds in ASP.NET Identity.
JWT signing: HMAC-SHA256 with key from AuthorizationConstants.JWT_SECRET_KEY.
When result.Succeeded is false, Token is an empty string in the response — not omitted.
No explicit HTTP status codes are set — returns 200 OK even when authentication fails (callers must check response.Result).
